title: Piquant Thai Fish Noodles
description: A fresh, aerated, adventurous Thai dish featuring fish with a flambé finish, crafted with paleo-friendly ingredients.
instructions: Rinse the shirataki noodle ribbons and pat dry, then soak them in hot water for five minutes to remove the odor.
Whisk the fish sauce, lime juice, grated ginger, chili paste and a pinch of salt to create the marinating sauce.
Toss the halibut pieces in the sauce and let them marinate for ten minutes.
Heat a wok over medium-high heat and add coconut oil, swirling to coat the surface.
Add minced garlic and sliced chili to the wok, stir-frying for thirty seconds until fragrant.
Place the marinated fish into the wok, cooking for two minutes on each side until the flesh turns opaque.
Add the soaked noodles to the wok, stir-frying for one minute, then pour the remaining sauce over the noodles and toss to coat evenly.
Return the cooked fish to the wok, drizzle the dark rum, ignite carefully, and allow the flame to subside before serving.
Garnish with fresh cilantro and toasted sesame seeds before plating.
